LABEL: anterior cervical intertransversarii muscle
Definition: A muscle of back that connects the anterior tubercles of adjacent cervical transverse processes, acting to produce lateral flexion of the cervical vertebral column and contributing to proprioceptive stabilization of the cervical spine; innervated by the anterior rami of cervical spinal nerves.

LABEL: anterior vertebral muscle
Definition: A muscle of neck that is a member of the prevertebral group situated between the prevertebral fascia and the cervical vertebral column, comprising the longus capitis, longus colli, rectus capitis anterior, and rectus capitis lateralis, innervated by cervical spinal nerve roots (C1-C8), and functioning primarily to flex and stabilize the head and neck at the craniocervical junction.

LABEL: articularis genu muscle
Definition: A skeletal muscle of the anterior thigh that arises from the anterior surface of the distal femoral shaft, deep to the vastus intermedius, and inserts into the synovial membrane of the knee joint; functions to retract the suprapatellar bursa and prevent synovial impingement during knee extension; innervated by the femoral nerve.

LABEL: circular pharyngeal muscle
Definition: A muscle of neck that forms the outer circular layer of the pharyngeal musculature, comprising the superior, middle, and inferior constrictor muscles, which encircle the pharynx and function to constrict the pharyngeal lumen during swallowing to propel the food bolus into the esophagus; innervated chiefly by the pharyngeal branch of the vagus nerve (CN X).

LABEL: clavicular head of pectoralis major muscle
Definition: A muscle head forming the superior portion of the pectoralis major, arising from the anterior surface of the medial half of the clavicle, whose fibers pass obliquely downward and laterally to insert onto the lateral lip of the bicipital groove of the humerus, and acting primarily to flex and medially rotate the arm at the shoulder joint, innervated by lateral pectoral nerve (C5–C6).

LABEL: dorsal part of intertransversarii laterales lumborum muscle
Definition: A lower back muscle that forms the dorsal subdivision of the lateral intertransversarii lumborum, connecting the accessory process of one lumbar vertebra to the transverse process of the adjacent vertebra below, contributing to lateral flexion and stabilization of the lumbar spine.
